name: Yatate
primary_sources: historical texts on Japanese writing tools

distinctive_features: ink compartment in the axis of the pen
chain-attached ink box used as a netsuke
shakudo alloy finish turning purple-black over time
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: long box with ink compartment and brush shaft, sometimes with a chain-attached ink box

domain_specific_functional.ink_storage: ink-saturated cotton in a sumi tsubo
domain_specific_functional.attachment_method: netsuke or tucked behind the belt

relationships.description: The yatate was inspired by traditional Chinese writing sets.
Yatate were used by samurai for writing and sometimes self-defense.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the evolution of portable writing tools in Japanese culture, associated with samurai and calligraphy.
primary_sources: historical texts on Japanese writing tools

contrastive_constraints: NOT a generic pen or pencil
